title: Park Naturalists
description: Plan, develop, and conduct programs to inform public of historical, natural, and scientific features of national, state, or local park.

tasks.text: Provide visitor services, such as explaining regulations, answering visitor requests, needs and complaints, and providing information about the park and surrounding areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist with operations of general facilities, such as visitor centers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with park staff to determine subjects and schedules for park programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct field trips to point out scientific, historic, and natural features of parks, forests, historic sites, or other attractions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Plan and organize public events at the park.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare and present illustrated lectures and interpretive talks about park features.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Plan, organize and direct activities of seasonal staff members.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform emergency duties to protect human life, government property, and natural features of park.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Train staff on park programs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop environmental educational programs and curricula for schools.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Construct historical, scientific, and nature visitor-center displays.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Research stories regarding the area's natural history or environment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare brochures and write newspaper articles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compile and maintain official park photographic and information files.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Take photographs and motion pictures for use in lectures and publications and to develop displays.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan and develop audio-visual devices for public programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform routine maintenance on park structures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide care for park program animals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Interview specialists in desired fields to obtain and develop data for park information programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Survey park to determine forest conditions and distribution and abundance of fauna and flora.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
